reviews.review_text: This is one of the better Caravan Parks I've ever stayed in. Friendly staff, Large leafy sites, lots of gardens and trees, clean and very tidy facilities and good value.
Nice clean little van park with excellent amenities
Everything was amazing had no complaints at all

A great little park, well maintained, clean and very tidy. The managers Jess and her hubby work very hard to keep it that way. Dog friendly which will always get out our big tick of approval (and our $) .
Staff at part are very nice to all tips of campers tents caravan rooftop tents all happy here
We enjoyed our visit very much staff provided excellent service couldn't be any more helpful we will be back
We were there with the caravan club only for 3 nights but were very impressed and happy with the park and the service there.
Beautiful spot to stay very relaxing will be going back soon as we can

We had a lovely stay on site 41 except for the sandflies.
The wind gave the awning a workout but it survived the test.
The staff were terrific and the amenities were spotless.
I even caught some muddies which was not only satisfying but tasty too.
Can’t wait to come back hopefully no sandflies.

Amazing holiday park. Quiet with a nice view of the water. Roomy sites, very clean amenities and friendly helpful staff. Short 200 metre walk to shops.Great meals at the lighthouse hotel and takeaway amazing bakery. Hairdressers, chemist, iga and  post office  Kangaroos and birds around for the nature enthusiasts.
Great place to stay. Quiet and lovely amenities. Friendly staff. I absolutely loved the community herb and veg garden and the book library.
Loved this little caravan park. Close walk to IGA cafes and great pub people were friendly 😀

Great park with very friendly managers
with nothing a problem

Had a few nights here recently. Great spot, clean, quiet, close to fishing facilities. We had a powered site which was manicured with nice green grass, walking distance to a pub, cafe and shops approx 100m, friendly staff and great value. Will definitely come back. If I was being overly critical, an extra dryer or two in the laundry would be great. Overall one of the best I've stayed at. Thanks.

Absolutely loved our stay here. We stayed in the Disability unit that was perfect for us. Plenty of room to get around and the two seperate bedrooms gave us more than enough space that we needed. There were plenty of cupboards in both bedrooms and kitchen area. Air Con in both bedrooms and in the common area. Bed so comfortable and it was golden silent on our stay at night. Looking out at the water from our long verandah was so peaceful.

I went with two small pets who needed shade and grass to feed on.  I was given a large grassed tent site out in full sun.  I asked for a shaded site and was given one but with no grass, only a dirty sandy site exposed to constant wind and showers during the stay.  The toilets were too far away during the night.  The camp kitchen was great, well equipped and with a community garden nearby.  The Laundry did need an upgrade with a newer clothes dryer especially as it rained a lot.  Very Clean Park and tennis courts as a bonus.  Its a great place for fishermen.  Atmosphere was old fashioned and quaint.
Stayed in a two-bedroom cabin (1). Bed was comfortable and everything was clean and tidy. Only issue was water was slow to drain from bathroom sink and shower.
Great spot to visit. Great facilities, that are clean and well kept. Short walk to shops for cafe, newsagent/post office, IGA, pub and coffee shop. Lovely walks around town and over to the water.
Cabin 5 is everything a person with a disability  needs Thoughtly designed love the pull down  lever on shower. Beautifully  done decor

We have been camping here for years and love the quiet, peaceful location. It's one of our favourite coastal spots!
Great cycling and walking paths, cafe, terrific fish and chip shop, good pub with excellent meals.
Easy run into Bargara and Bundaberg,  it really is a great place for a getaway.
The caravan park has a variety of sites, amenities block is old and needs to be refurbished, but is clean.  Only downsides are the increase in sandflies/mosquitoes, and the subtle increase of 'long term' campers.
Nice quiet spot
Management were very helpful. Ammenities clean and tidy and park in a great location. Will definitely come back and stay again.

We stayed on a powered site.
Sites are very spacious with concrete slab and a hedge  for privatisy.
The amenities were spotless as was the laundry.  We didn't  use the camp kitchen but it looked clean and tidy.
Nice walking paths and a walk up the street. Dog friendly. Couldn't  ask for more.
Management  was friendly.
We would stay here again if in the area

We stayed on a powered site, Jess let us check in early as we had battery problems.   Very kind and friendly.   Spotless amenities.   Walked to the Lighthouse Hotel for dinner and live music on the Saturday night.  Will definitely stay again.

We requested a grass van site and that suited our needs perfectly. Uninterrupted views across a grass paddock towards the Burnett River heads. Pumping yabbies directly out in front of the van park and a short distance to some fishing spots, great for young kids.
The park has clean facilities and friendly staff plus you can have small contained camo fires.
This was our 3rd visit and we’ll be back for more.
Great location within walking distance to shops, cafes etc. The park is neat and tidy. Amenities were clean. Staff were very helpful.
Awesome place to relax shops and cafe's close by well worth the visit

We booked in for a week back in February, extended an extra week, then stayed until after Easter. Total about 7 weeks.
What a fantastic park, managers and relieving managers are all great to deal with, very friendly and helpful.
Thanks to Jess, Ross, Fiona and Sam, y'all made it a great holiday.

They send info by email for park events on a regular basis, so we knew to be there and not miss out. Pizza night, weekly Fisho and other things that are happening.

Easter weekend had events in the park, unfortunately some people don't always partake, but it was a good time for those of us that attended.

Park is close to IGA, Pub, a few takeaways and a nice casual walk.
A longer walk to the boat ramp to drop a line if you feel the urge.

Amenities are excellent, always clean and tidy, great camp kitchen with everything one needs.

Laundry has plenty of machines to do the chores, books and DVD's to borrow while there too.
Several small vege garden beds with a variety of veges that can be picked if you like.

Some long-termers there, and they're always friendly and up for a chat.

Will definitely be back.

Gus and Kerrie Brisbane.

We had a wonderful week staying in our caravan. Nice park with clean amenities. Peaceful and close to shops. Fun family entertainment over the Easter weekend, us parents loved the colouring competition as we got a couple of hours a day with kids so keen to colour! Accommodating to changing our sites around to ensure our friends were next to us. Only thing is don't forget your bug spray, midges were our only issue.
Nice quiet spot with good facilities and nearby shops

Lovely spot, superb management who seem truly involved in making people's stay enjoyable. Terrible sand flies but you get that. Not really close to a beach but close enough if you don't mind a trudge. Really close to shops, nice coffee shop just around the corner, I'd give the coffee van a miss. Excellent amenities, a really family friendly spot. Totally worth a visit.
It would have a 5 🌟 review with a pool...
Beautiful clean cabin , clean park lovely friendly atmosphere . Kids activities , music and Easter bunny fantastic Hope to return again soon .
Caravan site

Staff is absolutely amazing.. they are very friendly and welcoming.. always go above and beyond to help out with anything.. they do a awesome job or cleaning and looking after the park grounds
For example we got decent rain while staying got flooded and everything got wet in our tent. the managers gave us credit for the dryers and washer which was unbelievable and helped so much And they even relocated us to a better spot that more suitable for our tent Absolute legends..
Highly recommend this spot 👌🏽😁
Definitely will be staying again
Perfect For our motorhome, close to everything. Very Clean amenities. Would recommend and will be returning.
Nice sites, friendly and helpful staff, very well priced.

We had a great stay on the weekend.
Very clean and comfortable cabins. Would definitely recommend to anyone needing accommodation in Burnett Heads.
Thank you again
The new caretakers are friendly, approachable and have improved the facilities of the park immensely in terms of cleanliness. We had a wonderful 7 nights.
Didn't use rooms
Perfect location,    secure,  peaceful and the serenity of nature right at our front steps.
Very nice little park, friendly and informative staff.
Great spot!

Great caravan park, good sized sites and trees to provide shade. Staff very friendly. I loved the vege gardens!
Perfect location to the cafe, shops, pub and IGA. Will definitely be back!
Beautiful well maintained caravan park!
As always, an excellent comfortable stay, great service, communication and location.
Great stay here for three months whilst we found a house.The park managers are great.Thankyou
A very lovely and peaceful place, cool breezes, clean amenities, and very friendly staff.We will go back
Fabulous little caravan park with an amazing amount of shady trees combined with plenty  of clear areas to charge rooftop solar. Pet friendly. Will definitely be back.
Lovely spot. Very peaceful. Cabin was immaculate. Highly recommend. Will definitely be back🙂

Nice quiet location, stayed in caravan park, amenities clean and looked after,
Didn't stay in rooms

We recently stayed in one of the cabins at Burnett Heads Lighthouse Holiday Park and had a wonderful experience. The cabin was spacious, comfortable, and thoughtfully designed, with great amenities and comfortable beds. The park itself is peaceful and relaxing, and the location is ideal — close to the port for our Lady Musgrave trip, a short drive to Bargara, and convenient to Bundaberg and all the local attractions. The only issue we had was that the twin beds weren’t made up with linen. Otherwise, everything was excellent and we thoroughly enjoyed our stay.

Just had 4 days here with a group of friends - highly recommend!
Great park with good clean amenities- hosts accommodating and attentive and park close to beach and a lovely walkway that takes you all the way along the coast.

We regularly stay here if we need to do a few things in Bundy. It is a great caravan park. Clean, tidy and extra added touches fruit trees and veggie gardens. All conveniences are nearby,  iga, fish n chips, pub, bakery, etc.
Great Van Park. Nice facilities and staff.
💯💯💯 great service. They know all there gear. Product are great.

Great service and location, great water pressure. Only downside is room wasn't cleaned very well prior to staying (hand prints on glass door, fridge not wiped out) but everything else was good though.

This caravan park is a perfect little beach front gem! We have stayed on a powered site twice now in the past 4 weeks. Ross & Jess are amazing hosts. They go out of their way to ensure you have the best experience here - the place is spotless in every way, picturesque, welcoming and one of the best we have stayed. We are hooked & so glad we found this park.
My cabin was lovely and clean and provided all that I needed ! The whole park itself was neat and tidy and the location and views are beautiful
We stayed here january 2026 in a cabin and it was immaculant! Also the lady in reception jess inparticular was amazing! I would stay here again for sure.
The location is awesome, the management is very helpful and friendly.
Beautiful quiet location. Close to the township, Mon Repos Turtle Centre, and Bundaberg. Spacious tent sites. Helpful management. We would come again :)
Great location, close to the water and the port for your charter boat! Amenities were tidy and well maintained, camp kitchen was good.
Absolutely 💯 quiet clean 👌 and awesome caretakers, cheers for the great holiday 😀 👍

Nice place to visit, will definitely be back. Nice and quiet. Few midges, but wind kept them away. Close walk into town. Would suit more adult only camping. We did not have kids, so we really enjoyed the peace and quiet. (If you have children Not much to do in Burnet heads itself for families.) but great small town if this is what you are after. Absolutely top spot to chill and do nothing!
Hosts were friendly, place nice and clean, landscaping tidy. Really only down side we would of like to have two key fobs for
Amenities use.
A great place for fishing, swimming and relaxing we will visit again! Loved it.

We loved our stay at the Burnett Heads Holiday Park! We spent 5 nights at the park between Christmas and New Years. We had a spacious tent site, and the park is in a great location, with clean amenities and a very laid-back vibe. Very family friendly and with lovely managers who even had a holiday program for the guests, with BBQ, live music, and face painting and movie night for the kids. Our 2 boys loved the place and playing with the kids from other camp sites. Definitely recommend and we will be back :-)
The staff are amazing.  I had 2nights really enjoyed.
The presentation of our cabin was exceptional. This is a credit to the park owners who were very welcoming and helpful with the park's facilities.

We stayed at Burnett Heads Holiday Park for a business trip, using both cabins and a caravan site. The park was clean, well maintained and very practical for a work stay. The cabins were comfortable, and the caravan site was spacious and easy to access. The new management has made such a positive difference to the park — everything was well organised, welcoming and running smoothly. It was a great place to unwind and relax. We would happily stay again and highly recommend it for both business and leisure stays.
A wonderful week of camping at Burnett Heads. Thank you Jess and Ross for the great activities organised for campers over the Christmas/New Year week. We had a ball.
Love this caravan park - great location & not too big. Lovely managers.

We enjoyed a beautiful stay over the Christmas holidays at Burnett Heads Holiday Park.
The staff were welcoming, rooms very tidy and location was great. It's the perfect spot for families and we will certainly be back!
Definitely a slice of paradise. Lovely and quiet, great facilities and exceptional staff.
Fabulous spot. Great clean facilities. Love the small size of the park allowing the kids to ride around safely. Amazing entertainment I wasn’t expecting. Thank you so much!
Awesome pet friendly park, being staying here for years and will continue to. Great customer service! Had a large powered slab site that was perfect for our family. Nice and shady but breezy too.
Great location for a quiet few days, clean amenities,  great service.  Will go back.
What an awesome amazing location such a well kept and maintained caravan and camping park it has everything you could possibly need and more
A place for overnight visits rather than week long stays, not much around or to do for young families.
This is a wonderful park and the caretakers do a wonderful job.  We will be back at sometime in the future.
Park was looked after well. Great gardens with produce to use, clean amenities and camp kitchen.
Just stopped overnight but was friendly, welcoming and very tidy. Well maintained.

Good park, in a great location, close to the Burnett River.  Amenities are good.
Very quite park. Well appointed camp kitchen.
A number of sites have hedges separating the sites, some have become overgrown and unsightly, need a heavy trim.  Otherwise a great little park.
Kicked out for taking a beer from a community fridge with no labelling or notice had 1 hour to leave
Great Park, very pleasant Managers, we had a Great stay all with walking distance from shops ect
Had a powered site, the view was very relaxing,and the staff extremely friendly and very helpful
Awesome little caravan park near the ocean.  Loved our stay.

Beautiful little piece of paradise. Our site was large and shady. The amenities were very clean and bathroom always smelt lovely. Great showers. The grounds are well kept and the staff are very friendly. Great location, just a quick walk to the main street. We had some lovely cucumbers, spring onions and radishes from the community garden in the Caravan Park. Would highly recommend and will definitely be back.

What a great little spot!  Roomy sites, shaded, close to the water but sheltered from the wind.   All amenities were clean and well maintained and the grounds were immaculate.  And peaceful, we were only there for 3 nights and are now wishing it was more.  We'll be back.
Lovely setting by the water. Will come again as we didn’t get a chance to use the tennis courts or many of the lovely surroundings,

This place is Great. Super Clean facilities and friendly managers. The common Kitchen area is well stocked with cooking ustensils and pans for every style of cooking.  The best part is the guest's gardens, were you can grab some greens to add to your meal. Keep in mind that it is not a free for all stocked fruit stand, just a small beautiful garden available for picking, to help your dishes shine a bit greener while on the road. Definitely a great place. Thank you!
Lovely place to stay. Ross and Jess are making the place look lovely. Clean amenities. Near the beach. Had a great steak down the pub for $19 !!

Lovely clean park and amenities.
Hosts are very friendly.
The park is shady and quiet, close to most facilities.
Professional service helpful in everyway made bookings no problem's, many thanks 👍.
Very quiet, nice location and friendly staff. Will return.

Always a great place to stay, location close to all facilities. Fantastic bike rides to Bargara through Mon Repos turtle area.
Be back next year for a couple of weeks.
We booked a great little on-site cabin which was very neat and clean.  Friendly staff and great amenities. Highly recommend if going to Lady Musgrave island on a tour.

This is a lovely caravan park, it has tres and shade that is rarely seen these days.
We love it for its location, river, ocean, bike path, hotel for great meals and basic shopping facilities. Best of all, it's all walking distance. Whilst we acknowledge that the amenities are old they are still reasonably maintained, my only criticism is the lack of cleaning by the managers, it seems the relieving managers do this but not the regular managers, to be honest, we stayed for 5 days and only saw them being cleaned the day we left. But all in all, a lovely park.
A lovely spot and friendly managers
New managers have certainly made our stay more enjoyable. Very friendly and approachable
Stayed for 4 nights, love the spot but hated the Sandflies... Amenities are a bit tired but clean...

We stayed 2 nites in an unpowered site. Extremely clean amenities, grounds, and camp kitchen. Professional, friendly staff, no troubles when trying to make up your mind on site location and rebooking. Recommend this park and would like to return one day.

Jess at reception was wonderful. We needed site's at very short notice as we had a family member very ill. Nothing was a problem for Jess she accommodated all of us and was very helpful,she told us that everything was fine if we needed to extend our stay at short notice. A big thank you to Jess.
Nice caravan park just ensure all your belongs are locked inside your van as we had our arc pit fire pit stolen off the back tyre of our caravan. The scumbag also tried to steel our bikes.

Our stay was fantastic, we were in our van. Good sites.
Amenities clean and good hot showers. Our second time at this park.
Thanks Team for a great stay,
Greg Fordham.
This is a lovely little gem. Big sites. Spotless amenities block with great water pressure. Five-minute stroll to pub, coffee, pizza, fish and chips, pharmacy and IGA. Quiet and relaxing.
I love this park. It’s quiet and relaxed and the new managers have enhanced that
Nice park, with shady sites. Close to the water. Clean amenities. Helpful staff.
Clean, friendly. Had a full kitchen including a fridge, tennis courts.
Very helpful staff, clean amenities, grounds tidy

Small caravan park. The amenities are good, has a camp kitchen and laundry.
There is NOTHING to do for kids in the park, however, there is a playground, skatepark and bmx track local to the area.

Wundervolle Lage, Ruhe, sehr freundliche Mitarbeiter und sowohl Campkitchen, wie auch Toiletten und Duschen sind gut. Nähe Monsoon Aquatics, eine Korallenaufzuchtsfarm, Bundaberg mit seiner Rum Destillerie und Bier Brauerei.
This was a very nice holiday park. We stayed in the villa there and it is worth every cent you spend, the beds are nice and also the bathroom was great. Would highly recommend coming here.

We saw other reviews on this site as low as 1. So when we booked it was with trepidation. The site is excellent. Yes there is no WiFi but who need it. The facilities are basic but clean. For the price point what more can you expect. We pitched on plot 35. Has to be the best. Parallel park to the fence and superb views across the flood plain to the estuary. The site staff are friendly. The pitches are level. The amenities are there it is a very short walk to Kelly's cafe which is also excellent. The village has a pharmacy a grocers and a liquor store. We really enjoyed the stay here.
The owners aren't very nice at all and it stunk of sewerage every day

We stayed there recently and it was nice however if I could give the service a zero I would. The female manager was very rude to me (we a smile on her face) and her husband yelled and swore at my husband in front of my 3 year old, which scared him.

This is an amazing little caravan park stayed for a week. Sites mostly had trees for shade. And concrete slabs. Great water pressure and water taste was pretty good. Bbq Everybody was very friendly and the caretakers were very helpful. Shower blocks and laundry were always clean and tidy. Showers NEVER ran out of hot water = amazing. Walking distance to an IGA, chemist, bakery, fish n chips shop, pizza, cafe and hotel. Oh and gym for those that require one. We had NO mozzies or miggies. Will definitely return again.

We did arrive late approx 6pm and did appreciate them taking us in on short notice. Although you could tell they werent happy.  However, when paying $69 for a caravan site I expected more. I have been too alot of caravan parks throughout Australia/Tasmania and I have never been to such an awful shower block. It is full of mould. It would take 5 minutes to spray 3 showers with exit mould to get rid of that problem.  The shower heads them self were beyond awful. I couldn't even wash my hair. Had to buy a jug to to and fill up to get the soap out. To top it off myself and my family got bitten by things and almost a week later the bites are still there and we are still so itchy from whatever it was. I just couldn't recommend coming here and unfortunately I won't be back.
Great locationfor holidays very Quiet

This place is awful, the amenities need an upgrade from the 50’s, the current caretakers don’t want to be there.
The gentleman nice the lady seems always annoyed, several commented she was rude.
With a couple of simple upgrades like new showers (especially shower heads and some pressure), access to water or beach out front, or putting in a pool would make this place worth $40 a night.

Lovely quiet park with grassy big drive through sites. We stayed on sites 30 & 31 which had a lovely shady tree to the east which gave us shade from the hot morning sun. There were also hedges on either side for privacy which was nice. Well kept park, tidy kitchen, old but clean and usable amenities. Short walk to pub and shops. Friendly staff. We would stay again, thanks for a lovely few days.
Superb site very clean with all amenities available. Reception was extremely helpful and told us all the places to go and whete the shops were, would recommend this a visit to this site.

We have enjoyed our stay at this park in the past, but this time around not so much. Overall, the location is great and the park itself is amazing, but management are unwelcoming and make you feel as though you are putting them out by simply arriving.

If you have kids, there is an amazing huge park a shirt walk away. The pub down the road is great, and the pizza shop makes great pizzas. There is also a small IGA for groceries.

We will return, but hope by then there is new management.

Very impressed. Location was perfect for us. The camp kitchen was excellent, plenty of electrical cooking appliances. Well stocked with cutlery and crockery. A fridge would make it better but I appreciate that this may be problematic. A small cosy park. Amenities extremely clean and tidy. Shops walking distance. A Great stay. We'll be back

Great little park and only a short walk to the IGA, bakery, takeaway shops, and the pub / tavern that offer great meals. The park is dog 🐕 friendly, neat and tidy. There are plenty of shady sites with slabs. Amenities are a bit old but spotless. Laundry is available at $4 per load (4 x $1). Park is in front of an old marina but it's only a short walk to the beach. Sites are reasonably priced, and the staff were friendly and helpful.

This is a clean and well maintained touring caravan park within easy walking distance of local shops and a pub in Burnett Heads.  A full range of facilities are available including dump point, well equipped camp kitchen, toilets and showers.
Always great to be here.

Great place to stop and relax every thing is within walking distance to town, pub shops fishing and the beach. Cheap rates and Don and Colleen are fantastic, even walk the park daily to say a brief GDay.

Super friendly welcome by park manager and her 4 legged friend. Reasonable price and fabulous location. Was a quiet location with handy facilities and easy walk near by. Gorgeous view from our little cabin and comfortable bed.||Bathroom could have been improved on cleanliness.
Great little park. Close to cafe, pub and IGA. Friendly staff in well kept park.
Lovely caravan park. Very nice managers 👌

Great small park. Clean amenities close to town wharf and beaches. Fantastic walking tracks great for bike riders. Close to Bundaberg and Bargara for day trips. Visit the Bundaberg run distillery it’s a great day out. Also the zoo in the gardens at bundy too

Absolutely disgusting behaviour by caretaker. Abused us for not parking in correct position. We had booked in for two nights. We left with a hard fight for refund by his wife who was responsible for the mix-up. She even admitted it.

Great quiet place to stay. Hot shower, well priced good size block. We will return here for sure. There’s a local pub and a very good IGA if you need.
Thanks Colleen

Recently stayed here and was really impressed.
The site is very well maintained and extremely clean.
Coupled with its location to shops & taverns within walking distance it was a very comfortable stay.
Looking forward to returning.
Great caravan park , small and quiet. Nice managers.
Nice quiet spot, clean amenities, well stocked camp kitchen and Close to shops and water.

Very nice quiet park.  Our hosts were very accommodating.  Park is kept spotlessly clean and tidy.  Great location, walking tracks to beaches next to park, Iga, bakery and Cafe.  Will definately return.

Great little caravan park. Awesome location walking distance to local shops and parks.
Caretakers are very helpful and friendly.
Clean amenities. Good location. Friendly staff.

Nice and quiet caravan park very close to the river mouth. We were given a great spot to put up the tent under a small shady tree. All the basic facilities we needed in the park were in good condition. The park is perfectly located in town - a brief walk to a cafe, IGA, pub and takeaway. We were there out of peak season so it may be much busier over Christmas time.

Lovely little campsite in a gorgeous area. Close to the local shops, pub and water front. Great option if visiting Bundaberg and wanting to stay some where a little quieter out of town. Only thing to bear in mind is the non powered sites are very small.

We were on our way with friends to Agnes Waters for three nights, and then to Yeppoon for three nights. Unexpectedly, both cars had breakdowns at different times, ours even had to be towed. We were lucky enough that Colleen and Dawn were able to accommodate us and we ended up staying for the whole week and not bothering about heading to Yeppoon. Fantastic location, great ammenities and we couldn’t be happier with how accommodating Colleen and Don have been. We, and our friends, certainly plan on returning.
Very friendly staff,close to town.

What an exceptional hidden gem!!! Very clean facilities,  very peaceful and Colleen and Don were extremely welcoming and friendly.  I would highly recommend staying here. We booked for three nights, ended up staying 7😁.

Ideal caravan park, we were in a camper and had lovely shady stand with water and electricity and a slab, the grounds are beautifully maintained and the kitchen is clean and full of everything you could need, the bathrooms are clean and spacious, the showers have lots of hot water and there is a good laundry too.  I highly recommend this place and. Colleen and Don are just so lovely and helpful.

Here on my second day of a 7 day booking, a seasoned traveller from WA to Sydney to the stunning Qld. And what a charming and delightful discovery.
Check in: Very professional and witty Lady who informed me precisely about the facilities within the park and surrounds, eg, the local pub allows you to dine with your beloved canine companions in the outer areas. Shops within 5 minute walk.
The park has beautifully kept tennis courts, a very peaceful short stroll to the waters edge and lovely lawns surrounding to exercise the furbabies with no prickles and friendly locals.
Extremely clean grounds, amenities and the most happy of people frequenting this place and working here. Plenty of smiles. It's peaceful yet not the least bit boring, the best greenery to divide the sites and provide shade. The evening lighting is perfect, not blinding. The camp kitchen is lovely and clean. So close to the marina, only 15 minutes drive from Bundaberg CBD, ticks all of the boxes in terms of, well, everything. Security with the boom gate and fob entry to amenities is a plus. A good mix of young and old. Birds chirping, people laughing, it's really the nicest of caravan parks and I'm very grateful to have found it.
A great park, great amenities, always spotless, good management friendly people, supermarket and pub close by. It is a council owned RV park, not a hotel
We stayed over in our caravan for a week. Highy reccomended! Very nice camping spots, spotless facilities and friendly and helpful hosts. Many thanks Colleen and Don for a great stay!

We thought this place was a little piece of paradise...until the guy came round demanding who's car the 2nd car was (hubby was working in bundy for a few days so we came down in separate cars) there was nowhere that specified 1 car per site & both cars fit so we didn't think anything of it! Until as I said he came around and demanded we move the 2nd car or we will be kicked out!  Came round a 2nd time, within half hour & they both shouted at us & threatened to kick us out, we didn't realise 40 mins until hubby was leaving was a make or break, as I said, hubby was going to work & would leave his car out when he got back...well that wasn't good enough for this pair, they then sent another person lol, but hubby's car was gone as we said it would be, this lady was actually decent though...so thank you :) the other pair were aggressive & wouldn't even stop shouting at us to listen to what we were saying & continued to shout!!
People across from us had their bikes stolen through the night & there is sandflies, amenities were clean & decent as was the camp kitchen!
I'd recommend it, the only dampener was the managers
Lovely location. Nice & peaceful. Easy walk to shops n cafe's. Very clean bathrooms. Tidy grounds. Excellent camp kitchen laundry & dog friendly. Lovely manager's

We booked into to this lovely park for 3 nights. Would have stayed longer but park is booked out for a music festival. Colleen and Don are lovely caretakers, and keep the park nice and clean. Sites are lovely and shady. I also bought some delicious local prawns. Would definitely recommend this park. Also pet friendly.  Ian and Pam
Great caravan park walk to the pub, iga, fish & chip shop

We stayed two nights in one of the little cabins very tidy and comfortable staff were very friendly and helpful surrounded by river and ocean veiws what a lovely place we thoroughly enjoyed it highly recomend a stay.

Very small pet friendly park. The waterfront sites are great drive-in drive out also great if you have a small boat but are very exposed to the wind and very dry and dusty. amenitys block is clean enough but extremely dated and desperately needs to be replaced .men's urinal is disgusting and always in the dark. sensor lights in the amenities are a joke lights should be on all the time and no disabled toilets. Overall a great little park always a pleasure to stay and a great location especially if you like walking and bike riding

The amenities are clean and the water hot. The sites are small and congested. You know that when other campers have move their rigs so you can reasonably maneuver to get into your spot - and then drag your van sideways to leave .. ..
The lighting at night is poor, we left our offside lights on at night to make it easier to see.
I know it's a council park, but I think they try and pack too many into the space they have.
I know some love it, but we'll not be back.

Didn’t enjoy the place one bit as the wind was always blowing a Gail. We have a outdoor kitchen on the van and we were placed on the windiest spot in the whole park, we were confronted by the manager about using our 2nd car as a wind break so we were able to cook but were rudely told to park the car out the front and that it was our problem not theirs, nice attitude I thought,  the neighbour next door had half our site using it as storage for his 4x4, boat & trailer, caravan plus annex but apparently that was acceptable. If you like being spoken to rudely by someone so miserable with no compassion whatsoever then this is the place to stay.. simply put…. I wil not be returning.
Nice clean caravan park

Very disappointed.  We wanted an unpowered site, we have great solar set up, but we're advised on phone our 15ft van was too big for unpowered sites but we could have a powered site at full price of course.  Drove by that afternoon and park was 3/4s empty.  You'd think anyone with any sense would advise you can park in powered site but not use power.  There loss of $25×14 days.
Close to coast and bike/walking path.

This is a lovely caravan park with a very pleasant, helpful onsite manager. It's well set out and spacious, near the water and so peaceful. Couple of things worth mentioning I think - firstly only one remote is handed out per van for amenities and gaining access to the park.  So, if one goes out in the car, the other is without entry to the amenities.  There is no wi-fi available and the ladies amenities has no power point.  The men's have two apparently.  I would think Bundaberg Regional Council could do better with this park.
Beautiful caravan park. Small and quiet.
No toiletries in the cabin disappointing.
